Activity Status.description: The status of an activity - one of an enumerated set of values.

Actors.description: Actors or Solution Roles related to this element.

Additional Properties.description: Additional Properties  allow arbitrary properties not defined in the type definitions to be added to any referenceable element.

Agreement End Date.description: Date when the agreement expires or was terminated, in ISO 8601 format.

Agreement Start Date.description: Date when the agreement becomes effective, in ISO 8601 format.

Category.description: A user-defined category for the element, used to group related elements for display or search purposes.

Collection Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the collection.

Confidence.description: A percent confidence in the proposed adding of the member.

Content Status.description: The lifecycle status of an element.

Display Name.description: The common name of an element.

Effective From.description: The beginning of when an element is viewable.

Effective Time.description: The time at which an element must be effective in order to be returned by the request.

Effective To.description: The ending time at which an element is visible.

Element Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the element being referenced.

Expression.description: An expression describing a membership, relationship or classification.

Extended Properties.description: A dictionary of properties that extend the properties of an entity type.

External Source GUID.description: The unique identifier of an external source.

External Source Name.description: The name of an external source

For Duplicate Processing.description: Flag indicating if the request is to support duplicate processing.

For Lineage.description: Flag indicating if the request is to support lineage.

Glossary Term.description: Term that provides meaning to this field.

GUID.description: A unique identifier - typically of an element in this context.

Is Own Anchor.description: A flag indicating if the element is its own anchor or is anchored to a different element.

Journal Entry.description: A text entry into a journal.

Label.description: A label used to identify or categorise a relationship link.

Merge Update.description: A flag indicating if the update should be a merge or replace.

Namespace.description: The namespace that qualifies the names of elements within this asset or data structure.

Namespace Path.description: The namespace path that qualifies the element's name within a larger naming hierarchy.

Notes.description: Notes and observations about the element.

Parent at End1.description: A flag indicating if the parent is at end1 of the relationship

Parent Relationship Attributes.description: A dictionary of relationship attributes to establish the parent relationship.

Qualified Name.description: The unique, text name of an element.

Request ID.description: A user provided or system generated request id for a conversation.

Resource Description.description: Description of the resource or how it is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the supporting resource in a ResourceList relationship.

Resource Properties.description: Additional properties needed to use the resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Use.description: Describes how the resource is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Security Tags.description: Optional security tags for security processing.

Start Date.description: Date at which the license or certification takes effect.

Status.description: Repository or element header status for an element.

Steward.description: The identifier of the steward responsible for the element.

Steward Property Name.description: Property name used to identify the type of the steward.

Steward Type Name.description: The type name of the steward element.

Summary.description: A short summary of the element's meaning or purpose.

Usage.description: The usage guidance for this element — how it is intended to be used in context.

User Defined Content Status.description: A user defined content status = only valid if content status is OTHER.

User Defined Status.description: A user-defined status value. Only valid when the primary status is set to OTHER.

Watch Resource.description: Whether the parent entity should receive notifications about changes to this resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Zone Membership.description: Zones scope visibility of elements to different users.

Access Control.description: The security access control endpoint in an AssociatedSecurityGroup relationship.

Business Imperatives.description: Defines the list of business imperatives the strategy supports.

Certification Type.description: The license type being used for the license.

Certified By.description: The name of the person or organization that issued the certification.

Certified By Property Name.description: The property name used to identify the certifier element (used with Certified By Type Name).

Certified By Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the element that issued the certification (used with Certified By Property Name to identify the certifier).

Child Zone.description: The child GovernanceZone in the zone hierarchy.

Class Word Classification.description: Classification from 0438. Applied to Referenceable. Marker classification (no attributes) indicating the element is a class word in a naming standard.

Confidence Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses ConfidenceLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, AD_HOC=1, TRANSACTIONAL=2, AUTHORITATIVE=3, DERIVED=4, OBSOLETE=5, OTHER=99.

Confidentiality Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses ConfidentialityLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, INTERNAL=1, CONFIDENTIAL=2, SENSITIVE=3, RESTRICTED=4, OTHER=99.

Criteria.description: The criteria for membership in a governance zone.

Criticality Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses CriticalityLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, MARGINAL=1, IMPORTANT=2, CRITICAL=3, CATASTROPHIC=4, OTHER=99.

Custodian.description: Custodian of the license or certification.

Custodian Property Name.description: The property name used to identify the custodian element (used with Custodian Type Name).

Custodian Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the element acting as custodian (used with Custodian Property Name to identify the custodian).

Data Collection End Time.description: End time for the data collection period.

Data Collection Start Time.description: Start time for the data collection period.

Deployed Implementation Type.description: The implementation type for a deploymed resource.

Document Identifier.description: A user supplied identifier for the governance document.

Domain Identifier.description: String representing the governance domain. All domains is ALL
Domain Identifier.Journal Entry:
**UML type:** int (GovernanceDomain enum)  
**Stored as:** string — convert to int downstream  
**Convention:** 0 = applies to all domains  
**See:** /Command Specifications/Governance Officer - Refactored/Docs/String-to-Int Conversion Register

Driver.description: The GovernanceDriver that initiates the governance response.

End Date.description: Date at which the license or certification expires.

Entitlements.examples: Allowed to use within the organization.
Entitlements.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing entitlements.

Governance Control 1.description: First GovernanceControl entity in the peer relationship.

Governance Control 2.description: Second GovernanceControl entity in the peer relationship.

Governance Driver 1.description: First GovernanceDriver entity in the peer relationship.

Governance Driver 2.description: Second GovernanceDriver entity in the peer relationship.

Governance Policy 1.description: First GovernancePolicy entity in the peer relationship.

Governance Policy 2.description: Second GovernancePolicy entity in the peer relationship.

Impact Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, severity_identifier: int}. severity_identifier uses ImpactSeverity enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, LOW=1, MEDIUM=2, HIGH=3, OTHER=99.

Implementation Description.description: Describes how this governance control is implemented.

Last Notification.description: The date of the last notification sent to this subscriber.

License Type.description: The license type being used for the license.

Licensed By.description: The name of the person or organization that granted the license.

Licensed By Property Name.description: The property name used to identify the licensor element (used with Licensed By Type Name).

Licensed By Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the element that granted the license (used with Licensed By Property Name).

Licensee Property Name.description: The property name used to identify the licensee element (used with Licensee Type Name).

Licensee Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the element receiving the license (used with Licensee Property Name).

Max Height.description: Maximum height of the vertical scope.

Max Latitude.description: Maximum latitude of the geographic scope.

Max Longitude.description: Maximum longitude of the geographic scope.

Measurement.description: A measurement for a governance metric.

Mechanism.description: The GovernanceControl that implements the governance mechanism.

Min Height.description: Minimum height of the vertical scope.

Min Latitude.description: Minimum latitude of the geographic scope.

Min Longitude.description: Minimum longitude of the geographic scope.

Minimum Notification Interval.description: The minimum interval between notifications in milliseconds.

Modifier Classification.description: Classification from 0438. Applied to Referenceable. Marker classification (no attributes) indicating the element is a modifier in a naming standard.

Monitored Resource.description: The Referenceable element being monitored by the governance control.

Multiple Notifications Permitted.description: Whether multiple notifications are permitted for this notification type.

Name Patterns.description: Name patterns for naming standard rules.

Next Scheduled Notification.description: The date of the next scheduled notification.

Notification Count.description: The number of notifications sent so far.

Notification Interval.description: The interval between scheduled notifications in milliseconds.

Notification Type.description: The NotificationType entity to link the subscriber to.

Obligations.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing obligations.

Operation Name.description: The name of the operation controled by the SecurityAccessControl.

Parent Zone.description: The parent GovernanceZone in the zone hierarchy.

Planned Completion Date.description: The planned completion date for the notification type.

Planned Start Date.description: The planned start date for the notification type.

Policy.description: The governance policy endpoint in a GovernanceResponse or GovernanceMechanism relationship.

Policy Management Point.description: Combined classification from 0435. Applied to Referenceable. Structure: {point_type: str, name: str, description: str}. point_type is one of: PolicyAdministrationPoint, PolicyDecisionPoint, PolicyEnforcementPoint, PolicyInformationPoint, PolicyRetrievalPoint. A Referenceable may have multiple policy management point classifications simultaneously.

Prime Word Classification.description: Classification from 0438. Applied to Referenceable. Marker classification (no attributes) indicating the element is a prime word in a naming standard.

Rationale.description: The rationale for using this control to  support this policy.

Recipient Property Name.description: The property name used to identify the certification recipient element (used with Recipient Type Name).

Recipient Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the element receiving the certification (used with Recipient Property Name).

Referenceable.description: The object being licensed or certified.

Regulation.description: The Regulation entity to link to the certification type.

Regulation Source.description: The source of the regulation - often, an authority.

Restrictions.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing restrictions.

Retention Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, basis_identifier: int, associated_guid: str, archive_after: date, delete_after: date}. basis_identifier uses RetentionBasis enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, TEMPORARY=1, PROJECT_LIFETIME=2, TEAM_LIFETIME=3, CONTRACT_LIFETIME=4, REGULATED_LIFETIME=5, TIMEBOXED_LIFETIME=6, OTHER=99.

Scope Elements.description: Map of element identifiers defining the scope of the data lens.

Security Group.description: The security group endpoint in an AssociatedSecurityGroup relationship.

Subscriber.description: The Referenceable entity subscribing to the notification type.

Supports Policies.existing_element: Policies - Governance Principles; Governance Obligations; Governance Approaches
Supports Policies.description: The policies that this governance control supports.

Usage Measurements.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing usage measurements.

Create Business Imperative.description: The BusinessImperative entity defines a business goal that is critical to the success of the organization.

Create Data Lens.description: A DataLens defines a geographic, temporal, and element scope for data collection and analysis.

Create Data Processing Purpose.description: Privacy regulations such as  (GDPR) require data subjects to agree the processing that is permitted on their data.

Create Governance Action.description: An executable action, or sequence of actions to support a governance requirement.

Create Governance Approach.description: The GovernanceApproach entity defines a policy that describes a method that should be used for a particular activity.

Create Governance Control.description: A governance control describes how a particular governance policy should be implemented. There are many types of controls.

Create Governance Driver.description: A motivating topic leading to governance work.

Create Governance Metric.description: A governance metric describes measurements that support governance requirements.

Create Governance Obligation.description: The GovernanceObligation entity defines a policy that describes a requirement that must be met.

Create Governance Policy.description: Policies  created in response to governance drivers. There are several types of policies.

Create Governance Principle.description: The GovernancePrinciple entity defines a policy that describes an end state that the organization aims to achieve.

Create Governance Procedure.description: A manual procedure that is performed under certain circumstances.

Create Governance Responsibility.description: A responsibility assigned to an actor or team. It could be a requirement to take a certain action in specific circumstances or to make decisions or give approvals for actions.

Create Governance Rule.description: An executable rule that can be deployed at particular points in the operations.

Create Governance Strategy.description: The strategy used in the development of the governance domains activities. How the governance domain supports the business strategy.

Create Governance Zone.description: A collection of assets that are used or processed in a specific way. Policies and controls can be attached to zones using the GovernedBy relationship.

Create Methodology.description: A Methodology describes a specific approach or framework for governance processes.

Create Naming Standard Rule.description: A standard for naming specific kinds of resources.

Create Notification Type.description: A NotificationType defines a type of notification that can be sent as a governance control.

Create Regulation.description: Defines a relevant legal regulation that the business operation must comply with. Often regulations are divided into regulation articles.

Create Regulation Article.description: A RegulationArticle entity is an article in a regulation. Dividing a regulation  simplifies planning and execution.

Create Security Access Control.description: A technical control that defines the access control lists that an actor must belong to be entitled to perform a specific action.

Create Security Group.description: A group of actors that need to be given the same access to a specific set of resources.

Create Service Level Objectives.description: Defines the performance, availability and quality levels expected by the element attached.

Create Terms & Conditions.description: A defined set of terms and conditions.

Create Threat Definition.description: The Threat entity describes a particular threat to the organization

Link Agreement T&C.description: Links an agreement to terms and conditions definition with implementation details.

Link Associated Group.description: Establishes that a Security Access Control applies to this Security Group.

Link Certification.description: Links a certification type to a referenceable element, adding details about the certification.

Link Governance Controls.description: Link peer governance controls with the GovernanceControlLink relationship. Controls types are: GovernanceRule, GovernanceProcess, GovernanceResponsibility, GovernanceProcedure, SecurityAccessControl, SecurityGroup.

Link Governance Drivers.description: Link peer governance drivers with the GovernanceDriverLink relationship. Drivers are: GovernanceStrategy, BusinessImperitive, Regulation, RegulationArticle, Threat.

Link Governance Mechanism.description: Link a governance policy to a governance control that supports it. The GovernanceMechanism relationship is used.

Link Governance Policies.description: Link peer governance policies with the GovernancePolicyLink relationship. Policies types are: GovernancePrinciple, GovernanceObligation, GovernanceApproach.

Link Governance Response.description: Links Policies as a response to governance Drivers.

Link Governed By.description: A referenceable element can be governed by one or more governance definitions.

Link License.description: Links a license type to a referenceable element, adding details about the license.

Link Monitored Resource.description: Links a GovernanceControl to a Referenceable that it monitors.

Link Notification Subscriber.description: Links a Referenceable as a subscriber to a NotificationType, defining the subscription parameters.

Link Regulation Certification Type.description: A certification type addressing a specific regulation.

Link Zone Hierarchy.description: Links child governance zones to parent governance zone.
